commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=013efb174e07d9d30b1c8cf19dc0c5d1caebc30a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds exec-driven Tailscale bring-up for Islo leases, records tailnet endpoints on claims, updates pond peer fallback behavior, and documents/tests the dual-plane behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects from source: the patch shows userspace Tailscale plus FeatureTailscale advertisement and omits existing exit-node/login-server flags. I did not run a live Islo tailnet setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should decide whether Islo userspace Tailscale may count as Crabbox's existing tailnet plane or needs a separate capability before automation tries to repair the branch.

target.title: feat(islo): add Tailscale plane to the islo provider
target.body:
## Summary

Islo is a delegated-run provider with no Crabbox-managed SSH lease, so an all-Islo pond could previously only use the **URL-bridge** plane — members were reachable by per-port HTTPS share URLs but never formed a true peer mesh. This PR gives the Islo provider a **Tailscale plane driven entirely from Crabbox's side** (no Islo-service or SDK changes).

When an Islo lease is warmed with `--tailscale`, Crabbox brings the sandbox onto the tailnet **through the existing Islo exec stream**:

1. downloads the pinned static Tailscale build (the image ships `wget`, not `curl`, and has no systemd);
2. starts `tailscaled` in **userspace-networking** mode — kernel mode rewrites the sandbox routing table and severs the Islo exec transport mid-run, so userspace mode is required and sufficient;
3. runs `tailscale up` with the pond-scoped advertise tags;
4. records the assigned tailnet IPv4 on the lease claim, so `pond peers` reports the member on `transport: tailnet`.

A lease warmed **without** `--tailscale` is unchanged: no tailnet IP is recorded and the member resolves on the URL bridge exactly as before. The existing pond ACL tag and its auto-bootstrap (`CRABBOX_POND_ACL_BOOTSTRAP=1` + `TS_API_KEY`) now apply to Islo like any other direct Tailscale-capable provider.

## Changes

- `internal/providers/islo/provider.go`: advertise `FeatureTailscale` (alongside `url-bridge`, `run-session`).
- `internal/providers/islo/tailscale.go` (new): the exec-driven bring-up + hostname/IP helpers.
- `internal/providers/islo/backend.go`: call the bring-up after sandbox creation; roll back sandbox + claim on failure.
- `internal/cli/claim.go` + `provider_exports.go`: new `UpdateLeaseClaimTailscale` helper so a delegated provider can record a tailnet endpoint (no SSH lease to go through `updateLeaseClaimEndpoint`).
- `internal/cli/pond_bridge.go`: a dual-plane provider reports `tailnet` when an endpoint is recorded, else falls back to the URL bridge instead of `pending`.
- Tests: `tailscale_test.go` (hostname/IP parsing), `TestPondPeersIsloDualPlane` and `TestPondPeersMixesIsloBridgeAndTailnetMembers` (discovery), updated capability assertions and the `testIsloProvider` stub.
- Docs + CHANGELOG.

## Config / secrets

- `CRABBOX_TAILSCALE_AUTH_KEY` — node auth key (tagged with, or authorized for, the pond tag). Existing flag/env; no new secret surface.
- `CRABBOX_ISLO_TAILSCALE_VERSION` — optional override for the pinned static Tailscale build (default `1.98.4`).

## Verification

```
go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox
go vet ./...
go test ./internal/providers/islo/... ./internal/cli/
```

Manual e2e against a live tailnet: a pond of **3 Islo sandboxes** warmed with `--tailscale` each joined the tailnet under the pond tag; `crabbox pond peers --pond mesh --json` reported all three on `transport: tailnet` with their `100.x` endpoints, and cross-box `tailscale ping` succeeded (relayed via DERP, as expected for NAT'd userspace nodes).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: zozo123
